congregation: Rows of believers in work clothes
static_blessing: May your noise floor be low
blackout_consequence: Eleven thousand generators came on, and a new aristocracy with them.
The refrigerated city spoiled first; the candle city did fine.
When the lights returned, several things that happened in the dark declined to un-happen.
Three neighborhoods never fully reconnected, by accident or by invoice.
flood_consequence: The water left in a week. The insurance men never came at all.
They found the seawall money three years later, in a marina upstate.
The neighborhood learned to read tide charts like racing forms.
Some blocks pumped out. Some blocks learned to float.
flood_graffiti: HIGH GROUND IS A LANDLORD'S WORD
surge_flotsam: three sealed shipping containers nobody claimed
the old pier, returned in pieces
a vending machine full of dry cigarettes
transit_graffiti: THE L TRAIN LIVES (IN OUR HEARTS)
fire_consequence: The hydrants worked on the rich side of the block.
Arson, said the marshal. Insurance, said everyone else.
The block rebuilt in vinyl and resentment.
Three companies fought it; one of them set it.
tabloid_heat: FRY AN EGG ON THE MAYOR
crash_consequence: The ATMs worked; the numbers in them didn't.
Midtown wept into its phones. The outer boroughs, long broke, barely noticed.
Cash came back like an old fashion. So did the men who guard cash.
Six banks became two banks became a question.
housing_consequence: The landlords blinked first, that once.
Forty marshals came on a Tuesday. The drum line was louder.
The settlement was sealed; the new boilers weren't.
It spread to nine buildings before winter broke it.
street_memorial: candles and a basketball jersey, renewed weekly
a wall of names repainted brighter each year
plastic flowers zip-tied to a lamppost, fresh ones every month
war_consequence: Both crews lost their founders and kept their accountants.
The truce line runs down the middle of the avenue to this day, and so does the price difference.
Forty-one funerals, two parades, one extremely careful peace.
It ended the way they end: everyone tired, nothing learned, territory identical.
signal_shrine: a satellite dish repainted gold, aimed at nothing in the sky anyone can find
a ring of dead phones, screens up, kept charged by hand
an antenna grown over with morning glories, still humming
water_shrine: a high-water line traced in blue paint and pearl buttons
a rowboat filled with candles, dry for years now
a font of harbor water, changed daily, never explained
miracle_shrine: an intersection where the candles never blow out, verified twice
a painted saint whose eyes locals repaint every dawn before anyone can check them
a fire escape where the pigeons land in the same formation every dusk
miracle_desc: a stopped clock that chimed the hour of the blackout, once, correctly
rain that fell on one side of the street for nine days
a mural whose painted window showed a light that no one painted
every cat on the block facing the river at the same moment, photographed
vision_desc: a door at the end of a subway platform that opens onto warm light
the harbor, perfectly still, with all the bridges gone
an apartment they had never rented and could draw from memory
a voice reading station names that do not exist, in order
schism_reason: whether the second broadcast had already happened
the treasurer, the treasury, and a marina upstate
whether converts from the old faiths needed re-baptism in harbor water
a single comma in the founding tenet
relic_examples: subway tokens from the last train
water from the '31 flood, sealed in baby food jars
riot_ending: with the visiting bench barricaded in their own locker room
in three hours of joy that did a million dollars of damage
when the league pulled the refs out by helicopter, which everyone agreed was excessive
with the trophy missing for nine days and returned polished
radio_content: station names, read slowly, some of them real
weather reports for years that haven't happened
a woman reading debts aloud and declaring them void
music nobody can Shazam and everybody hums
exodus_graffiti: LAST ONE OUT FEED THE CATS
exodus_count: more than the census would admit
